trigger: LLM generated timestamp fields using datetime.utcnow().isoformat() assuming downstream consumers would treat them as UTC
symptom: Mnemosyne/Qdrant rejects records or silently stores wrong timestamps; temporal queries return wrong ordering; 'naive datetime' warnings in logs
good_code:
from datetime import datetime, timezone
# worker.py
record = {
    'id': job_id,
    'created_at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),  # timezone-aware
    'content': result_text,
}
notes: datetime.utcnow() returns a naive datetime with no tzinfo. APIs that expect ISO 8601 with Z/+00:00 will either reject it or silently misinterpret. PEP 495 / Python 3.12 deprecates utcnow(). Fix: datetime.now(timezone.utc).
